title: vepris oubanguensis
intro: Vepris oubanguensis, also known as the Congo orange or Cameroon orange, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its essential oil, which has been reported to exhibit antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Vepris oubanguensis include its potential in treating fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its use in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and antiparasitic properties. Herbal preparations of Vepris oubanguensis typically involve the use of the plant's leaves, bark, or roots, which are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
